stemHtml:
A jet aeroplane has three configurations available for take-off.

T/O 1: 1 stage of slats and flaps
T/O 2: 2 stages of slats and flaps
T/O 3: 3 stages of slats and 3 stages of flaps

The runway is NOT limiting but the given departure requires a climb gradient of 6.8% to 2000 ft. Which T/O configuration will be most appropriate to achieve the best climb gradient?

options.a: T/O 3 because it results in greater climb speeds and greater rate of climb than when using T/O1.
options.b: T/O 1 because it results in lower climb speeds, giving a higher angle of climb than when using T/O2.
options.c: T/O 2 because it results in lower climb speeds and greater rate of climb than when using T/O3.
options.d: T/O 1 because it results in greater climb speeds and greater rate of climb than when using T/O2.
